and data science specialist. About me.

Deep Learning With Keras And Tensorflow

Dec 5, 2015

Google, through your subsidiary Alphabet, has announced its latest machine learning engine called TensorFlow as being open-sourcing. After that, I decided to go through it and analysing if it’s worth to invest my time on using that on my projets. So, let’s to the first question:

What’s TensorFlow?

TensorFlow is an software library for numerical computation using data flow graphs. We can use it for tasks such as language translation, image and speech processing. It has a flexible architecture that can be deployed to single or multiple CPUs and GPUs within systems ranging from smartphones to large server arrays as needed. TensorFlow has been developed within Google for its own uses, though it’s now to shared within an Open Source community.

What’s Keras?

Keras is a minimalist, highly modular neural networks library, written in Python and capable of running either on top of either TensorFlow or Theano. It was developed with a focus on enabling fast experimentation. Being able to go from idea to result with the least possible delay is key to doing good research. Keras has been built focused on allows an easy and fast prototyping (through total modularity, minimalism, and extensibility) for deep learning projects. It supports both convolutional networks and recurrent networks, as well as combinations of the two, supports arbitrary connectivity schemes (including multi-input and multi-output training) and runs seamlessly on CPU and GPU.

In this article we had an overview about the framework that I’m going to use on my project. On the next articles, I will describe how to use them at a more technical level.

Volunteering

I’m often hearing people talking about work-life, personal life and how they try to balance it. Ok, few years ago, I was in the same group. Then, something great happened into my life: I started to travel. Somehow, who I was never came back from the first trip. I couldn’t see the world as the same way, I realized that the world is not so big as we usually think and each person has something in common to each other, doesn’t matter the difference of culture.
